**Source** [Gamemastery Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=22) pg. 118

**Onset**
1 day

**Stage 1** [clumsy 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3) (1 day)

**Stage 2** clumsy 1 and a –5-foot status penalty to Speed (1 day)

**Stage 3** clumsy 2 and a –10-foot status penalty to Speed (1 day)

</column>
name: Bog Rot

<title level="1" right="Disease 8" pfs="">[Nightmare Fever](/Diseases.aspx?ID=12)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Disease" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=46" />
<trait label="Necromancy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=117" />
<trait label="Occult" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=120" />
</traits>

**Source** [Gamemastery Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=22) pg. 119

Thought to be caused by a [night hag’s](/Monsters.aspx?ID=257) curse, nightmare fever inflicts you with terrible nightmares, and you awaken with the wounds you received in your dreams. Some versions cause you to dream of being wounded by bludgeoning or piercing weapons, in which case you take that type of damage instead. Damage and the [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15) condition caused by the disease can’t be healed until the disease is removed.

<column gap="tiny">

**Saving Throw** DC 25 Will

**Stage 1** 2d6 slashing damage and fatigued (1 day)

**Stage 2** 4d6 slashing damage and fatigued (1 day)

**Stage 3** 4d6 slashing damage, fatigued, and whenever you take slashing damage, you must succeed at a Will save against the disease’s DC or become [frightened 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19) (1 day)

**Stage 4** 6d6 slashing damage, fatigued, and whenever you take slashing damage, you must succeed at a Will save against the disease’s DC or become [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28) for 1 round (1 day)

**Stage 5** 6d6 slashing damage and [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38)

</column>
name: Nightmare Fever

<title level="1" right="Disease 11" pfs="">[Brain Worms](/Diseases.aspx?ID=13)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Disease" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=46" />
<trait label="Virulent" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=162" />
</traits>

**Source** [Gamemastery Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=22) pg. 119

Scholars suspect these brain parasites have an otherworldly or extraplanar origin. Though transmitted by the bites of infected targets, the disease remains relatively rare—most hosts are killed by the effects before they can pass it on. While infected, whenever you attack due to confusion, you bite your target (if you don’t have a jaws or fangs attack, you deal piercing damage as an unarmed attack with damage equal to your lowest unarmed attack).

<column gap="tiny">

**Saving Throw** DC 28 Fortitude

**Onset**
1 day

**Stage 1** [stupefied 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) (1 day)

**Stage 2** stupefied 2, and whenever you take damage, you must succeed at a Will save against the disease’s DC or become [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=5) for 1 round (1 day)

**Stage 3** stupefied 3, and whenever you take damage, you must succeed at a Will save against the disease’s DC or become confused for 1 minute (1 day)

**Stage 4** stupefied 4 and confused, damage does not end the confused condition (1 day)

**Stage 5** [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) (1 day)

**Stage 6** death

</column>
name: Brain Worms

<title level="1" right="Disease Level Varies" pfs="">[Addiction](/Diseases.aspx?ID=15)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Gamemastery Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=22) pg. 120

Track the maximum stage you reach with each drug’s addiction. This maximum stage is separate from your current addiction stage for the drug. The maximum stage can’t be reduced, even if you fully remove the disease. When you take the drug, two things happen: you attempt a saving throw against addiction, and you suppress the effects of addiction for 1 day. Failing a save against addiction caused by taking the drug causes you to go to 1 stage higher than the maximum stage you had previously reached (2 stages higher on a critical failure). If you’re currently suffering from addiction when you attempt a save from taking the drug, you can’t improve your stage; if you succeed at the save, the stage remains the same as it was.

 When you attempt your save against addiction each week, the stage you are currently at can’t get worse—it can only stay the same or improve. The conditions from addiction can’t be removed while you are affected by the addiction, and suppressing addiction by taking the drug only avoids the effects—it doesn’t remove the disease.

<column gap="tiny">

**Saving Throw** Fortitude (DC equals that of the drug)

**Onset**
1 day

**Stage 1** [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15) (1 week)

**Stage 2** fatigued and [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) (1 week)

**Stage 3** fatigued, [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10), and sickened 1 (1 week)

**Stage 4** fatigued, drained 2, sickened 2, and [stupefied 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) (1 week)

</column>
name: Addiction
saving_throw: Fortitude (DC equals that of the drug)

<title level="1" right="Disease 15" pfs="">[Blightburn Sickness](/Diseases.aspx?ID=21)</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Extinction Curse</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Disease" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=46" />
</traits>

**Source** [Pathfinder #155: Lord of the Black Sands](/Sources.aspx?ID=33) pg. 73

The ceiling of the vault of the Black Desert is studded with deadly crystals that glimmer like stars above the dark sands. These crystals are radioactive and flood the vault with lethal energy. Creatures other than those native to the vault or who have survived there for a long time develop a disease called blightburn sickness.

Creatures native to the Black Desert are immune, as are creatures who are affected by blightburn sickness but recover from it. The target can't recover from the disease's [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10) or [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) condition except by magic.

 The surest way to avoid contracting blightburn sickness is by wearing a [_blightburn ward_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=653), a piece of protective jewelry often found among [urdefhans'](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=158) gear or for sale in Shraen.

<column gap="tiny">

**Saving Throw** DC 32 Fortitude

**Onset**
1d4 days

**Stage 1** drained 1 (1 day)

**Stage 2** drained 1 and sickened 1 (1 day)

**Stage 3** drained 2 and sickened 2 (1 week)

**Stage 4** drained 3 and sickened 3 (1 month)

**Stage 5** increase drained condition by 1 (1 year)

</column>
name: Blightburn Sickness
primary_source: Pathfinder #155: Lord of the Black Sands
primary_source_raw: Pathfinder #155: Lord of the Black Sands pg. 73

<title level="1" right="Disease 15" pfs="">[Addictive Exhaustion](/Diseases.aspx?ID=23)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Disease" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=46" />
</traits>

**Source** [Monsters of Myth](/Sources.aspx?ID=107) pg. 65

Addictive exhaustion results from exposure to [Kuworsys's](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1733) spores, through close proximity with the monster or in fungus-filled pits throughout Kuworsys's lair. At the onset, afflicted creatures find themselves gradually becoming disinterested in life. As the disease worsens, they become hyper-focused on a single activity. As the disease reaches its final stages, sustenance and rest provide no relief for them.

<column gap="tiny">

**Saving Throw** Fortitude; creatures with addictive exhaustion can't reduce their [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) condition from addictive exhaustion while they have the disease

**Stage 1** The creature is sickened 1. One skill of the creature's choice doesn't suffer the status penalty from this sickened condition to its skill checks (1 day)

**Stage 2** As Stage 1, but the creature is sickened 2 and is [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15) (5 days)

**Stage 3** As Stage 2, but the creature is sickened 3 and has incurable hunger and thirst, in addition to being fatigued (1 day)

</column>

<title level="1" right="Disease 2" pfs="">[Rust Creep](/Diseases.aspx?ID=25)</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Rusthenge</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Disease" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=46" />
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=48" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

**Source** [Rusthenge](/Sources.aspx?ID=215) pg. 19

Those afflicted by rust creep develop uncomfortable rust-colored bruises on their flesh and endure full-body aches like those one might experience after a long workout. As the affliction progresses, their bodies—as well as the clothing and items they wear or carry—increasingly break down until a painful death occurs. If a character successfully resists contracting rust creep, or recovers from a case of rust creep, they are temporarily immune to future rust creep infections for 24 hours.

<column gap="tiny">

**Saving Throw** DC 15 Fortitude

**Stage 1** –1 status penalty to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) checks (1 day)

**Stage 2** as stage 1 (1 day)

**Stage 3** [enfeebled 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13) (1 day)

**Stage 4** enfeebled 1 and [stupefied 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37), plus any armor, clothing and items you carry and that are of a level equal to or less than the disease become [broken](/Conditions.aspx?ID=2) as the decay spreads to them (1 day; broken items remain broken)

**Stage 5** [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) (1 day)

**Stage 6** unconscious (1 day)

**Stage 7** death

</column>
name: Rust Creep

1 week

**Stage 3** [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=73), can’t recover from the fatigued condition, and coughing requires a successful DC 15 flat check to Cast a Spell that has the concentrate trait or Activate an Item that has the concentrate trait (1 week)

**Stage 4** [unconscious](/Rules.aspx?ID=378) (1 week)

**Stage 5** death

</column>

**Stage 3** enfeebled 3, fatigued, and take 1d6 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) every 1d20 minutes (1 day)

</column>

<title level="1" right="Disease 11" pfs="">[Brain Worms](/Diseases.aspx?ID=38)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Disease" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=578" />
<trait label="Virulent" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=726" />
</traits>

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 89

Scholars suspect these brain parasites have an otherworldly or extraplanar origin. Though transmitted by the bites of infected targets, the disease remains relatively rare—most hosts are killed by the effects before they can pass it on. While infected, whenever you attack due to confusion, you bite your target (if you don’t have a jaws or fangs attack, you deal piercing damage as an unarmed attack with damage equal to your lowest unarmed attack).

<column gap="tiny">

**Saving Throw** DC 28 Fortitude

**Onset**
1 day

**Stage 1** [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 2 (1 day)

**Stage 2** stupefied 2, and whenever you take damage, you must succeed at a [Will save](/Rules.aspx?ID=3241) against the disease’s DC or become [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=63) for 1 round (1 day)

**Stage 3** stupefied 3, and whenever you take damage, you must succeed at a Will save against the disease’s DC or become confused for 1 minute (1 day)

**Stage 4** stupefied 4 and confused, damage does not end the confused condition (1 day)

**Stage 5** [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95) (1 day)

**Stage 6** death

</column>

An extended respiratory disease, tuberculosis can pose particular challenges to spellcasters and some performers due to the intense coughing fits it produces.

1 week

**Stage 3** [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15), can’t recover from the fatigued condition, and coughing requires a successful DC 15 flat check to Cast a Spell with a verbal component or Activate an Item with a command component (1 week)

**Stage 4** [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) (1 week)

**Stage 5** death

</column>

**Stage 2** drained 2 (1 day)

**Stage 5** mutation (on a failure) or death (on a critical failure)

</column>

A pernicious disease spread by bloodsucking insects, malaria sometimes enters long periods of dormancy. If you succumb to malaria, you may continue to be periodically affected by the disease, even if you’re cured. You can’t reduce your [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) condition while affected with malaria.

<column gap="tiny">

**Saving Throw** DC 16 Fortitude

**Onset**
10 days

**Stage 1** sickened 1 (1 day)

**Stage 2** sickened 2 (1 day)

**Stage 3** sickened 2, and disease recurs every 1d4 months even if cured (1 day)

**Stage 4** [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) (1 day)

**Stage 5** death

</column>

<title level="1" right="Disease 3" pfs="">[Bubonic Plague](/Diseases.aspx?ID=6)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Disease" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=46" />
</traits>

**Source** [Gamemastery Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=22) pg. 118

This widespread illness can sweep through entire communities, leaving few unaffected. The first indication of the disease is a telltale swelling of glands. In some cases, the disease can move into your lungs (pneumonic plague) or blood (septicemic plague), which is even more fatal. If you have bubonic plague, you can’t remove the [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15) condition while affected.

<column gap="tiny">

**Saving Throw** DC 17 Fortitude

**Onset**
1 day

**Stage 1** fatigued (1 day)

**Stage 2** [enfeebled 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13) and fatigued (1 day)

**Stage 3** enfeebled 3, fatigued, and take 1d6 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) every 1d20 minutes (1 day)

</column>
name: Bubonic Plague

<title level="1" right="Disease 4" pfs="">[Scarlet Leprosy](/Diseases.aspx?ID=7)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Disease" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=46" />
<trait label="Virulent" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=162" />
</traits>

**Source** [Gamemastery Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=22) pg. 118

Scarlet leprosy is widely feared for its devastating effects, crushing bones and organs while making recovery nearly impossible. Damage taken from scarlet leprosy can’t be healed until the disease is cured.

<column gap="tiny">

**Saving Throw** DC 19 Fortitude

**Onset**
1 day

**Stage 1** 2d6 bludgeoning damage (1 day)

**Stage 2** 2d6 bludgeoning damage, and whenever you gain the [wounded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=42) condition, increase the condition value by 1 (1 day)

**Stage 3** 4d6 bludgeoning damage and can’t heal any Hit Point damage (1 day)

</column>
name: Scarlet Leprosy

<trait label="Primal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=134" />
</traits>

**Source** [Gamemastery Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=22) pg. 118

If you are wounded and exposed to persistent cold, you might contract bonechill.

<column gap="tiny">

**Saving Throw** DC 20 Fortitude

**Onset**
1 day

**Stage 1** [clumsy 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3) (1 day)

**Stage 2** clumsy 2 and can’t heal cold damage until this disease is cured (1 day)

**Stage 3** clumsy 3 and all [cold temperature effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=642) are one step more severe for the victim (1 day)

**Stage 4** [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28) and all cold temperature effects are one step more severe for the victim (1 day)

</column>

**Stage 3** drained 2 and can’t speak (1 day)

**Stage 4** death

</column>
